description: Eval tests for /rune:strive multi-agent work execution skill

evals.expected_output: The skill should parse the plan, create a team, spawn workers, execute tasks, run quality gates, and cleanup the team.
expectations: TeamCreate is called with team_name starting with 'strive-'
Plan is parsed into 3 tasks matching the plan structure
Workers (rune-smith and/or trial-forger) are spawned via Agent tool
TeamDelete is called after workflow completes
Completion report includes task execution summary

evals.expected_output: Tasks with dependencies should be serialized via blockedBy relationships. Task 2 should wait for Task 1 to complete.
expectations: Task dependencies are detected from plan 'Depends on' field
Tasks with dependencies have blockedBy set correctly
Task 2 is blockedBy Task 1 (Database Schema)
Task 3 is blockedBy Task 2 (Auth Service)
Dependent tasks execute sequentially, not in parallel

evals.expected_output: The skill should detect ambiguity and ask clarifying questions before proceeding with implementation.
expectations: Ambiguous task descriptions are detected in Phase 0
AskUserQuestion is called for clarification
Clarification questions are specific to the ambiguous content
No Agent spawns occur before user confirmation
User response is incorporated into task definitions

evals.expected_output: Tasks that modify the same file should be serialized via blockedBy to prevent conflicts.
expectations: File ownership conflict is detected for src/api/users.py
All three tasks targeting the same file are identified
Tasks are serialized via blockedBy relationships
inscription.json task_ownership reflects the serialization
Tasks execute sequentially, not concurrently

evals.expected_output: Should warn about committing to main branch and offer to create a feature branch.
expectations: Branch check detects current branch is main
Warning is displayed about committing to main/master
AskUserQuestion offers branch creation option
Feature branch is created if user approves
Workflow continues on new branch after user confirmation

evals.expected_output: Should auto-detect and offer the most recent plan for execution.
expectations: Plans directory is scanned for available plan files
Available plans are listed or most recent is selected
AskUserQuestion presents plan options to user
User selection is respected and correct plan is loaded

evals.expected_output: Should create isolated git worktrees for each worker, enabling parallel work without index conflicts.
expectations: --worktree flag is detected and activates worktree mode
git-worktree skill is loaded for worktree management
merge broker is used instead of commit broker
Workers execute in isolated worktrees
Worktrees are cleaned up after workflow completes

evals.expected_output: Should run quality gates (lint, typecheck, test) after implementation and report results.
expectations: Quality gates are discovered from package.json/Makefile/pyproject.toml
All discovered wards are executed (lint, typecheck, test)
Ward results are included in completion report
Failed wards trigger fix task creation
Fix worker is spawned to address ward failures

evals.expected_output: Each task should require user approval of implementation proposal before coding begins.
expectations: --approve flag activates proposal approval flow
Worker writes proposal to tmp/work/*/proposals/*.md
Proposal is presented to user via AskUserQuestion
Proposal includes Approach, Files to Modify, Files to Create, Risks sections
Implementation only starts after user approval

evals.expected_output: Should clean up team and resources even when workers crash or fail.
expectations: Worker crash is handled gracefully
TeamDelete is called even on error
Partial progress is reported in completion report
Stale team directories are cleaned up
Workflow lock is released on exit
